raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/XXXX I received a fraud alert from Barclays Bank on my XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX card for charges made in XXXX. The same day I received a letter from Barclays confirming a change of address- which I did not make. I called Barclays, we reviewed the charges, all of which were fraudulent and they closed the card and took off the charges. Important to note - the number of the card was not the number of my card. I never applied for this card, never received it, and never made these charges. One month later, I received notification that the charges were put back on my card because the card had a chip and it was in my presence. Again- this is a card with a different number that I never applied for or received. I called and explained and Barclays fraud said they would investigate. I just received two letters dated XX/XX/XXXX that the charges were valid because I had the card in my presence and they were sending my name to credit reporting agencies if I did not pay. I have letters noting cards with XXXX different numbers - Neither of which I applied for, received or used. Barclays refuses to investigate this.
